## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

Hands off to fe-verify-requirements when done.

- caveman:caveman-compress
- caveman:caveman-help
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-stats
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard: Launch the interactive web dashboard to visualize a codebase's knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review
- security-review

content.text:
Two things I have to flag up front, because they affect whether the cron job keeps working:

1. **The token expires.** Your JWT's `exp` is `1784345326` → **2026-07-18**. A cron job will start getting `401 SESSION_EXPIRED` after that. There's no refresh/login endpoint I know of, so the script reads the token from a `.env` file you can update. I'll make it email you an alert when the token dies rather than fail silently.
2. **Sending email needs a *sender* account.** `kid21200@gmail.com` is the recipient. To send, the script needs SMTP credentials (e.g. a Gmail account + [app password](https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords)). I'll wire it through env vars — you fill them in once.

Let me build it (pure stdlib, so cron just needs system Python).

#!/usr/bin/env python3 """Fetch AI-for-Vietnam problem statements and email a summary. Pulls every page from the /problem-statements/browse endpoint, keeps four fields per problem (Track, Title, Sponsor, Slots) and emails the result. Config is read from environment variables, or a `.env` file sitting next to this script (so it works from cron without a login shell). Required keys: AIFV_TOKEN Bearer token for the API SMTP_USER Sender email account (e.g. you@gmail.com) SMTP_PASS SMTP password / Gmail app password Optional keys (with defaults): EMAIL_TO = kid21200@gmail.com EMAIL_FROM = SMTP_USER SMTP_HOST = smtp.gmail.com SMTP_PORT = 587 API_BASE = https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org FINGERPRINT = af16af0d43075bd31d940e4b262badb8 """ from __future__ import annotations import json import os import smtplib import ssl import sys import urllib.error import urllib.request from email.message import EmailMessage from email.utils import formatdate from pathlib import Path SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent PAGE_SIZE = 50 # API max def load_env() -> None: """Load KEY=VALUE lines from a sibling .env into os.environ (no override).""" env_path = SCRIPT_DIR / ".env" if not env_path.exists(): return for raw in env_path.read_text().splitlines(): line = raw.strip() if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line: continue key, _, val = line.partition("=") key = key.strip() val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'") os.environ.setdefault(key, val) def cfg(key: str, default: str | None = None) -> str: val = os.environ.get(key, default) if val is None: raise SystemExit(f"Missing required config: {key}") return val def fetch_page(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str, page: int) -> dict: url = f"{base}/problem-statements/browse?page={page}&page_size={PAGE_SIZE}" req = urllib.request.Request( url, headers={ "accept": "application/json", "authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "origin": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org", "referer": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org/", "x-client-fingerprint": fingerprint, "user-agent": "aifv-problem-fetcher/1.0", }, ) with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp: return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8")) def fetch_all(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str) -> list[dict]: """Page through the endpoint until a short page signals the end.""" items: list[dict] = [] page = 1 while True: data = fetch_page(base, token, fingerprint, page) batch = data.get("items", []) items.extend(batch) if len(batch) < PAGE_SIZE: break page += 1 return items def slim(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]: """Keep only the four requested fields.""" rows = [] for it in items: rows.append( { "track": it.get("track", ""), "title": it.get("title_en") or it.get("title_vi") or "", "sponsor": it.get("sponsor_name", ""), "slots": f"{it.get('slots_left', '?')}/{it.get('slot_cap', '?')}", } ) return rows def build_text(rows: list[dict]) -> str: lines = [f"AI-for-Vietnam problem statements — {len(rows)} total", ""] for i, r in enumerate(rows, 1): lines.append(f"{i}. [{r['track']}] {r['title']}") lines.append(f" Sponsor: {r['sponsor']} | Slots: {r['slots']}") return "\n".join(lines) def build_html(rows: list[dict]) -> str: head = ( "" "#Track" "TitleSponsor" "Slots" ) body = [] for i, r in enumerate(rows, 1): body.append( f"{i}{r['track']}{r['title']}" f"{r['sponsor']}{r['slots']}" ) return ( f"

AI-for-Vietnam problem statements — {len(rows)} total

" "" f"{head}{''.join(body)}
" ) def send_email(subject: str, text: str, html: str | None = None) -> None: smtp_user = cfg("SMTP_USER") smtp_pass = cfg("SMTP_PASS") msg = EmailMessage() msg["Subject"] = subject msg["From"] = os.environ.get("EMAIL_FROM", smtp_user) msg["To"] = os.environ.get("EMAIL_TO", "kid21200@gmail.com") msg["Date"] = formatdate(localtime=True) msg.set_content(text) if html: msg.add_alternative(html, subtype="html") host = os.environ.get("SMTP_HOST", "smtp.gmail.com") port = int(os.environ.get("SMTP_PORT", "587")) ctx = ssl.create_default_context() with smtplib.SMTP(host, port, timeout=30) as server: server.starttls(context=ctx) server.login(smtp_user, smtp_pass) server.send_message(msg) def main() -> int: load_env() base = os.environ.get("API_BASE", "https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org") fingerprint = os.environ.get("FINGERPRINT", "af16af0d43075bd31d940e4b262badb8") token = cfg("AIFV_TOKEN") try: items = fetch_all(base, token, fingerprint) except urllib.error.HTTPError as e: detail = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace") subject = f"[AIFV] Fetch FAILED ({e.code})" body = ( f"Could not fetch problem statements.\n\n" f"HTTP {e.code} {e.reason}\n{detail}\n\n" "If this is 401/SESSION_EXPIRED, the bearer token in .env expired — " "grab a fresh token from the hub and update AIFV_TOKEN." ) send_email(subject, body) print(body, file=sys.stderr) return 1 except Exception as e: # network, JSON, etc. subject = "[AIFV] Fetch FAILED (error)" body = f"Could not fetch problem statements.\n\n{type(e).__name__}: {e}" send_email(subject, body) print(body, file=sys.stderr) return 1 rows = slim(items) text = build_text(rows) html = build_html(rows) send_email(f"[AIFV] Problem statements — {len(rows)} total", text, html) print(f"Sent {len(rows)} problems to {os.environ.get('EMAIL_TO', 'kid21200@gmail.com')}") return 0 if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main())

Here's the direct link to create a Gmail App Password (the 16-character `SMTP_PASS`):

**https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords**

A few things to know so it actually works:

1. **2-Step Verification must be ON first.** The App Passwords page only appears if 2FA is enabled. Turn it on here if needed: **https://myaccount.google.com/signinoptions/two-step-verification**
2. On the App Passwords page, type any name (e.g. `aifv-cron`) → **Create**.
3. Google shows a **16-character** password like `abcd efgh ijkl mnop`. Copy it and **remove the spaces** when pasting into `.env`.
4. Put it in `.env` as the sender account's password:
   ```
   SMTP_USER=your-sender@gmail.com
   SMTP_PASS=abcdefghijklmnop
   ```
   `SMTP_USER` is the account you generated the password *from* (the sender) — it can be `kid21200@gmail.com` itself if you want it to email itself, or any other Gmail you control.

If the App Passwords link says "not available," it's almost always because 2FA isn't on yet, or the account is a Workspace account where the admin disabled it.

Pulls every page from the /problem-statements/browse endpoint, keeps four fields per problem (Track, Title, Sponsor, Slots) and emails the result. Config is read from environment variables, or a `.env` file sitting next to this script (so it works from cron without a login shell). API max def load_env() -> None: """Load KEY=VALUE lines from a sibling .env into os.environ (no override).""" env_path = SCRIPT_DIR / ".env" if not env_path.exists(): return for raw in env_path.read_text().splitlines(): line = raw.strip() if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line: continue key, _, val = line.partition("=") key = key.strip() val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'") os.environ.setdefault(key, val) def cfg(key: str, default: str | None = None) -> str: val = os.environ.get(key, default) if val is None: raise SystemExit(f"Missing required config: {key}") return val def fetch_page(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str, page: int) -> dict: url = f"{base}/problem-statements/browse?page={page}&page_size={PAGE_SIZE}" req = urllib.request.Request( url, headers={ "accept": "application/json", "authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "origin": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org", "referer": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org/", "x-client-fingerprint": fingerprint, "user-agent": "aifv-problem-fetcher/1.0", }, ) with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp: return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8")) def fetch_all(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str) -> list[dict]: """Page through the endpoint until a short page signals the end.""" items: list[dict] = [] page = 1 while True: data = fetch_page(base, token, fingerprint, page) batch = data.get("items", []) items.extend(batch) if len(batch) < PAGE_SIZE: break page += 1 return items def slim(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]: """Keep only the four requested fields.""" rows = [] for it in items: rows.append( { "track": it.get("track", ""), "title": it.get("title_en") or it.get("title_vi") or "", "sponsor": it.get("sponsor_name", ""), "slots": f"{it.get('slots_left', '?')}/{it.get('slot_cap', '?')}", } ) return rows def build_text(rows: list[dict]) -> str: lines = [f"AI-for-Vietnam problem statements — {len(rows)} total", ""] for i, r in enumerate(rows, 1): lines.append(f"{i}. " "" f"{head}{''.join(body)}
" ) def send_email(subject: str, text: str, html: str | None = None) -> None: smtp_user = cfg("SMTP_USER") smtp_pass = cfg("SMTP_PASS") msg = EmailMessage() msg["Subject"] = subject msg["From"] = os.environ.get("EMAIL_FROM", smtp_user) msg["To"] = os.environ.get("EMAIL_TO", "kid21200@gmail.com") msg["Date"] = formatdate(localtime=True) msg.set_content(text) if html: msg.add_alternative(html, subtype="html") host = os.environ.get("SMTP_HOST", "smtp.gmail.com") port = int(os.environ.get("SMTP_PORT", "587")) ctx = ssl.create_default_context() with smtplib.SMTP(host, port, timeout=30) as server: server.starttls(context=ctx) server.login(smtp_user, smtp_pass) server.send_message(msg) def main() -> int: load_env() base = os.environ.get("API_BASE", "https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org") fingerprint = os.environ.get("FINGERPRINT", "af16af0d43075bd31d940e4b262badb8") token = cfg("AIFV_TOKEN") try: items = fetch_all(base, token, fingerprint) except urllib.error.HTTPError as e: detail = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace") subject = f"[AIFV] Fetch FAILED ({e.code})" body = ( f"Could not fetch problem statements.\n\n" f"HTTP {e.code} {e.reason}\n{detail}\n\n" "If this is 401/SESSION_EXPIRED, the bearer token in .env expired — " "grab a fresh token from the hub and update AIFV_TOKEN." ) send_email(subject, body) print(body, file=sys.stderr) return 1 except Exception as e: # network, JSON, etc. subject = "[AIFV] Fetch FAILED (error)" body = f"Could not fetch problem statements.\n\n{type(e).__name__}: {e}" send_email(subject, body) print(body, file=sys.stderr) return 1 rows = slim(items) text = build_text(rows) html = build_html(rows) send_email(f"[AIFV] Problem statements — {len(rows)} total", text, html) print(f"Sent {len(rows)} problems to {os.environ.get('EMAIL_TO', 'kid21200@gmail.com')}") return 0 if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Fetch AI-for-Vietnam problem statements and email a summary.

Pulls every page from the /problem-statements/browse endpoint, keeps four
fields per problem (Track, Title, Sponsor, Slots) and emails the result.

Config is read from environment variables, or a `.env` file sitting next to
this script (so it works from cron without a login shell). Required keys:

    AIFV_TOKEN     Bearer token for the API
    SMTP_USER      Sender email account (e.g. you@gmail.com)
    SMTP_PASS      SMTP password / Gmail app password

Optional keys (with defaults):

    EMAIL_TO       = kid21200@gmail.com
    EMAIL_FROM     = SMTP_USER
    SMTP_HOST      = smtp.gmail.com
    SMTP_PORT      = 587
    API_BASE       = https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Dump every problem statement to problems/<id>-<slug>.md with full detail.

Reuses config + fetching helpers from fetch_problems.py. Lists all problems
via the browse endpoint, then fetches each one's detail (which adds
detailed_description and attachments) and writes a markdown file per problem.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import re
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

import fetch_problems as fp

OUT_DIR = fp.SCRIPT_DIR / "problems"


def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    return re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", text).strip("-")[:60] or "problem"


def fetch_detail(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str, pid: int) -> dict:
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{base}/problem-statements/{pid}",
        headers={
            "accept": "application/json",
            "authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
            "origin": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org",
            "referer": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org/",
            "x-client-fingerprint": fingerprint,
            "user-agent": "aifv-problem-fetcher/1.0",
        },
    )
    import json

    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))


def clean(text: str | None) -> str:
    return (text or "").replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n").strip()


def to_md(d: dict) -> str:
    title = d.get("title_en") or d.get("title_vi") or f"Problem {d['id']}"
    lines = [
        f"# {title}",
        "",
        f"- **ID:** {d.get('id')}",
        f"- **Track:** {d.get('track', '')}",
        f"- **Sponsor:** {d.get('sponsor_name', '')}",
        f"- **Slots:** {d.get('slots_left', '?')}/{d.get('slot_cap', '?')}"
        f"{' (FULL)' if d.get('is_full') else ''}",
    ]
    title_vi = d.get("title_vi")
    if title_vi and title_vi != title:
        lines.append(f"- **Title (VI):** {title_vi}")
    lines.append("")

    summary = clean(d.get("description"))
    if summary:
        lines += ["## Summary", "", summary, ""]

    detail = clean(d.get("detailed_description"))
    if detail:
        lines += ["## Detailed Description", "", detail, ""]

    attachments = d.get("attachments") or []
    if attachments:
        lines += ["## Attachments", ""]
        lines += [f"- {a}" for a in attachments]
        lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"


def main() -> int:
    fp.load_env()
    base = os.environ.get("API_BASE", "https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org")
    fingerprint = os.environ.get("FINGERPRINT", "af16af0d43075bd31d940e4b262badb8")
    token = [REDACTED]("AIFV_TOKEN")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    listing = fp.fetch_all(base, token, fingerprint)
    print(f"{len(listing)} problems listed")

    written = 0
    for item in listing:
        pid = item["id"]
        detail = fetch_detail(base, token, fingerprint, pid)
        title = detail.get("title_en") or detail.get("title_vi") or ""
        path = OUT_DIR / f"{pid}-{slugify(title)}.md"
        path.write_text(to_md(detail), encoding="utf-8")
        written += 1
        print(f"  wrote {path.name}")

    print(f"Done: {written} files in {OUT_DIR}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import re
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

import fetch_problems as fp

OUT_DIR = fp.SCRIPT_DIR / "problems"


def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    return re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", text).strip("-")[:60] or "problem"


def fetch_detail(base: str, token: str, fingerprint: str, pid: int) -> dict:
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{base}/problem-statements/{pid}",
        headers={
            "accept": "application/json",
            "authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
            "origin": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org",
            "referer": "https://hub.aiforvietnam.org/",
            "x-client-fingerprint": fingerprint,
            "user-agent": "aifv-problem-fetcher/1.0",
        },
    )
    import json

    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))


def clean(text: str | None) -> str:
    return (text or "").replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n").strip()


def to_md(d: dict) -> str:
    title = d.get("title_en") or d.get("title_vi") or f"Problem {d['id']}"
    lines = [
        f"# {title}",
        "",
        f"- **ID:** {d.get('id')}",
        f"- **Track:** {d.get('track', '')}",
        f"- **Sponsor:** {d.get('sponsor_name', '')}",
        f"- **Slots:** {d.get('slots_left', '?')}/{d.get('slot_cap', '?')}"
        f"{' (FULL)' if d.get('is_full') else ''}",
    ]
    title_vi = d.get("title_vi")
    if title_vi and title_vi != title:
        lines.append(f"- **Title (VI):** {title_vi}")
    lines.append("")

    summary = clean(d.get("description"))
    if summary:
        lines += ["## Summary", "", summary, ""]

    detail = clean(d.get("detailed_description"))
    if detail:
        lines += ["## Detailed Description", "", detail, ""]

    attachments = d.get("attachments") or []
    if attachments:
        lines += ["## Attachments", ""]
        lines += [f"- {a}" for a in attachments]
        lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"


def main() -> int:
    fp.load_env()
    base = os.environ.get("API_BASE", "https://hub-api.aiforvietnam.org")
    fingerprint = os.environ.get("FINGERPRINT", "af16af0d43075bd31d940e4b262badb8")
    token = [REDACTED]("AIFV_TOKEN")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    listing = fp.fetch_all(base, token, fingerprint)
    print(f"{len(listing)} problems listed")

    written = 0
    for item in listing:
        pid = item["id"]
        detail = fetch_detail(base, token, fingerprint, pid)
        title = detail.get("title_en") or detail.get("title_vi") or ""
        path = OUT_DIR / f"{pid}-{slugify(title)}.md"
        path.write_text(to_md(detail), encoding="utf-8")
        written += 1
        print(f"  wrote {path.name}")

    print(f"Done: {written} files in {OUT_DIR}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

# Advanced RAG Knowledge Base

- **Track:** finance_banking
- **Sponsor:** SHB Bank

## Summary

SHB manages thousands of internal policies and external regulatory documents that are interconnected through cross-references, amendments, partial replacements, and evolving legal validity. The challenge is to build an AI-powered knowledge assistant that understands these complex document relationships and delivers accurate, up-to-date, and source-cited answers to users' natural language queries.

## Detailed Description

An advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that enables employees and customers to search across the bank's entire document repository using natural language. The system covers internal policies, regulations, circulars, operating procedures, contract templates, and more.
What sets this solution apart is its ability to handle the complexities of real-world banking documentation:
Cross-references: The system automatically follows and synthesizes information from related documents, so users get complete and context-aware answers.
Amendments: The system always applies the latest effective version of amended regulations, ensuring responses reflect current rules.
Partial supersession: When regulations are partially updated, the system excludes superseded clauses from responses, keeping information accurate and relevant.
Conflicting regulations: The system detects inconsistencies between documents and alerts users, helping them avoid confusion and compliance risks.

Done.

- **Now:** the every-15-min notifier is **disabled** — its line is gone from the crontab, so no more posts until morning.
- **07:30 today (Saturday):** the `30 7 * * *` entry runs `enable_cron.sh`, which re-installs the 15-min job and then removes its own line — so it fires exactly once and the crontab goes back to just the notifier.
